commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7742)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter determinism: remove source-aware post-Prettier patches (§3.2)

Remove preserveBannerSpacingGaps and preserveTrailingNewlineForVerbatimTopLevelMultilineBlockComment from format-entry.ts.

Both functions violated target-state.md §3.2 (Formatter Boundary & Allowlist):

- preserveBannerSpacingGaps inspected `source` regex patterns to
  conditionally insert blank lines before banner-comment sequences in the
  formatted output. This made formatting non-deterministic: two files with
  identical logical structure but different surrounding whitespace produced
  different output.

- preserveTrailingNewlineForVerbatimTopLevelMultilineBlockComment returned
  `source` verbatim (bypassing the formatted result) when Prettier's only
  change was adding a trailing newline. Returning raw source from a
  formatter is a prohibited recovery fallback per §3.2: "The formatter must
  never attempt recovery or fallback printing."

format() is now a thin, deterministic wrapper: it delegates entirely to
prettier.format() and returns the result unchanged.

Updated test-banner.output.gml to remove the two blank lines that were
previously injected by preserveBannerSpacingGaps. Added two §3.2 boundary
tests to formatter-boundaries-ownership.test.ts verifying source-
independence and no-verbatim-source-return. Updated multiline-block-comment-
formatting.test.ts to reflect that the formatter now adds a trailing newline
instead of returning source verbatim.

Security Update (#331)

* Security Update

Identified and remediated 15 vulnerabilities across the skill-validator
runtime, dashboard, and CI/CD pipelines.

Changes:
- Runtime (AgentRunner.cs): path traversal prevention, deny-by-default
  permissions, env scrubbing, MCP server hardening
- Regex (AssertionEvaluator.cs): ReDoS timeout
- Logging (SkillDiscovery.cs): exception logging
- Dashboard (dashboard.html, dashboard.js): SRI hash, XSS escaping
- CI/CD (5 workflow files): SHA pinning, persist-credentials: false
- Tests (RunnerTests.cs): 45+ new security test cases

MCP Server Defense-in-Depth (5 layers):
1. Command allowlist: only dotnet, node, npx, python, python3, uvx
2. Path rejection: no /, \, or .. in command names
3. Args validation: per-runtime dangerous flag blocklist
4. Env sanitization: strips PATH, LD_PRELOAD, NODE_OPTIONS,
   DOTNET_STARTUP_HOOKS, etc.
5. Cwd dropped: custom working directories not passed to SDK

Subprocess Isolation:
- Sensitive env vars + prefix patterns scrubbed from child processes
- 120-second timeout with Kill(entireProcessTree: true)
- Deny-by-default permission model

Supply Chain:
- All GitHub Action references pinned to full commit SHAs
- All checkout steps set persist-credentials: false
- Chart.js CDN pinned to v4.4.7 with SRI integrity hash

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786) (#1492)

* fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786)

When go-to-definition is used on a type that lives in a different
assembly from the one FCS reports (e.g. System.Boolean forwarded from
netstandard.dll to System.Runtime.dll), ILSpy's CSharpDecompiler
throws:
  'Decompiling types that are not part of the main module is not supported'

This happens because ILSpy's type system resolves type forwarders
transparently — GetTypeDefinition returns the actual type definition
from the target assembly's module, but the decompiler was created for
the source assembly. The mismatch causes the exception.

Fix: after resolving the type definition, check whether its
ParentModule matches the decompiler's main module. If not (a type
forwarder), retrieve the actual assembly path from
IModule.PEFile.FileName and create a fresh CSharpDecompiler for
that assembly. Resolve the type definition again in the new type
system, then decompile as normal.

If the PEFile path is unavailable or the type cannot be resolved in
the redirected assembly, the original decompiler and type definition
are used unchanged — the existing try/catch will handle any remaining
failure gracefully.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

Distinguish STANDARD and ADVANCED inference levels in Clue agent (#226)

* Make cascade inference ADVANCED-only; medium difficulty uses immediate inference

Medium (STANDARD) agents now only check against current knowledge at the
moment a suggestion comes in, rather than re-examining every previous
suggestion when new info is learned. Cascade inference (replaying the full
suggestion history) is reserved for hard (ADVANCED) difficulty, creating a
meaningful gap between the two levels.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01N7BcbQnmRYpgdFNUHXeQiC

* Use multiprocessing instead of single-threaded asyncio in tournament runner

Replaces asyncio.gather (one core, one event loop) with multiprocessing.Pool
so games spread across all CPU cores. Each worker runs batches of games with
its own asyncio event loop and fakeredis instance.

Results: 4x speedup on 4 cores (107s vs 438s for 1000 games, 9.3 vs 2.3
games/sec). Adds --workers/-w flag to control parallelism.

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine

feat: store timezone on events instead of inferring from creator (#121)

Events now persist their timezone at creation time rather than looking up
the creator's profile timezone. This fixes issues where:
- Creator changes their profile timezone after creating an event
- Creator is deleted (was falling back to first attendee's timezone)
- Creator travels to a different timezone

- Add timezone column (varchar, NOT NULL, default 'America/Los_Angeles')
  to events table with migration that backfills existing rows
- Save form-selected timezone when creating/editing events
- Reminder webhooks use event.timezone directly (removed leftJoin on
  users table that PR #119 added)
- Event edit route now shows timezone selector and uses event's stored
  timezone for time conversion
- InlineTimezoneSelector gains onChange callback prop
- Email notifications still format per-recipient timezone (unchanged)

which caused name collisions for projects that share the same directory
name and mixed unrelated concerns in one cache tree.

  var (agents_dir/logs); DEV_WORKTREE drops the REPO_ROOT.name segment

  config-derived path without overriding ORC_LOG_FILE/ORC_LOG_DIR env vars

fix(dispatcher): only dispatch QA when coder signals done via close_task.sh

Previously any commit on a feature branch (including WIP commits) would
trigger QA dispatch. This caused QA agents to be spawned prematurely
when coder-N had uncommitted or work-in-progress commits.

Root cause: the fallback in _derive_task_state() returned 'qa' for all
unrecognised commit messages, and route() in the state machine mapped
CODER_WORK to 'qa' alongside CODER_DONE.

Fix:
- state_machine.py: CODER_WORK now routes to 'coder' (still working);
  only CODER_DONE routes to 'qa'
- git.py: fallback returns 'coder' with a 'not yet signalled done'
  message instead of dispatching QA
- Updated all affected tests; added QA-context test to reach 100%
  coverage of the new 'elif agent_name == "qa"' branch
